What Happens: WNBA leadership/Board of Governors states it will consider new markets, tied to a target team count.
League sets a deadline; ownership groups file applications covering financing, arena, market data.
Scored on ownership strength, arena/practice facility readiness, corporate & media support, women's sports track record.
NBA and WNBA owners jointly vote to approve specific cities and ownership groups.
Typical Timeframe: One vote; 2-4 yrs lead time to debut

What Happens: New owners pay the league a lump fee. Risen from $50M (2025) to $250M (2028-30).
Team hires GM, coach, business ops staff; secures practice facility and arena lease.
Existing teams protect a set number of players; new team(s) draft unprotected players.
New team participates in the next college draft, typically without the very top pick.
Team debuts; league monitors attendance/ratings/revenue to inform future expansion.
